pattern: Enumerate every subset of the word list with bitmask iteration; for each subset check letter availability by summing character frequencies and comparing against the letter supply; track the maximum valid subset score.
hints: With at most 14 words, there are at most 2^14 = 16384 subsets — enumerate all of them directly rather than searching.

For each subset, tally the total frequency of every letter needed by the chosen words and compare that against the available supply from the letters list; if any letter is over-budget, the subset is invalid.
Accumulate the per-letter score for each valid subset using the score array indexed by the character's ordinal, and keep the running maximum across all valid subsets.

constraints.note: each word has length 1..15, lowercase letters only
each element is a single lowercase letter
exactly 26 elements, one per lowercase letter a-z

edge_inputs.description: overlapping letter demands — only one high-value word can be chosen because both need the same scarce letter
single word that exactly exhausts the supply
all words are individually affordable but together exceed the supply — greedy by score fails
no letter has a non-zero score, so the answer must be 0 regardless of which subset is chosen
one word, insufficient letters — result is 0
maximum word count — 14 words that each need a distinct letter, all affordable
choosing the subset with individually highest-scoring words is suboptimal compared to a larger subset of lower-scoring words

stress.description: 14 words of length 10 drawn from a restricted alphabet — many subsets are valid, stress-tests the full 16384 enumeration
stress.note: all 14 words affordable individually; tight supply forces subset search to find the real maximum

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maxScoreWords(self, words, letters, score):
        from collections import Counter
        # brute-force: try every subset via recursive enumeration
        supply = Counter(letters)
        n = len(words)
        best = 0
        def bt(i, avail, current_score):
            nonlocal best
            if i == n:
                best = max(best, current_score)
                return
            # skip word i
            bt(i + 1, avail, current_score)
            # try to use word i
            needed = Counter(words[i])
            for ch, cnt in needed.items():
                if avail.get(ch, 0) < cnt:
                    return
            new_avail = dict(avail)
            word_score = 0
            for ch, cnt in needed.items():
                new_avail[ch] = new_avail[ch] - cnt
                word_score += score[ord(ch) - ord('a')] * cnt
            bt(i + 1, new_avail, current_score + word_score)
        bt(0, dict(supply), 0)
        return best
